headlines.title: TEXAS CATTLEMEN TO FICHT REPEAL
headlines.subtitle: Shipment for Immediate Slaughter From Tick Infected Territory Desired.
headlines.fullText: Texas cattlement through the Cattle Raisers' Association and in mass meetings are protesting against the repeal et the act that permits the shipment et cattle to market for immediate slaughter from tick Infected territory. R. H. Harris, vice president of the Cattle Halsers' Association, has called on the cattlemen to meet in San Angelo Monday at 2 p. m. to protest against the recommendation made by Secretary Houston. Sam H. Cowan, general attorney for the association, says the recommendation would mean a tremendous expense to the cattlemen of Texas and Oklahoma and would work an injustice. headlines.title: HEAD OF ART SCHOOL TO GIVE LECTURE HERE
headlines.subtitle: Frank Alvah Parsons Will Speak at Library Feb. 20.
headlines.fullText: Frank Alvah Parsons, president of the School of Fine and Applied Arts. New York, will lecture in Fort Worth at Carnegie Public Library Friday afternoon. Feb. 20. He will come under the auspices of the Fort Worth Art Association, His subject will be. "Art In Modern Life." Parsons gave a delightful lecture manage on "The How and Why of an remembered for free as well as for its practicality. He is one of the best known lecturers on art In America. Parsons will be in Texas as one of visiting lecturers the walking lecturer for Home Economics, Texas, Feb. 2-14. During Home Economics Week at the university he will lecture on the following topics: "The Present Social and Economic Meaning of Art" "Color-A Modern Language "The New Home," of Expression," "The Ethics and Aesthetics of Modern DressOFArt and the General Home Economie Problem. Parsons has a warm spot in his heart for Fort Worth, and likes to stop here whenever Grace Fort Fakes, one is of the home of the most talented Star students produced by the sea tom now a teacher in that Institutionstes HOW Teacher Sts Wert Seventh street, and the niece of W. G. Turner.

headlines.title: Progressive Democrats Plan 'Common Counsel' Clubs
headlines.subtitle: Folk Takes Lead in Organization Which Holds Biweekly Meetings to Discuss Issues.
